title: MineChromium
description: Adds a Picture-In-Picture Browser window using mcef
body:
### 🌐 PIP Browser – In-Game Picture-in-Picture Web Browser
MineChromium adds a fully functional Chromium-powered web browser to Minecraft, viewable as a draggable HUD overlay. Browse wikis, watch videos, follow maps, or keep chats open—all without leaving the game.
![PIP Youtube window](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/593501410d9bfac4ae17b5851eb03a8e9e99e2b5.png)

### ✨ Features
In-Game Web Browser: Full MCEF browser with Back/Forward/Refresh and quick Google access.
Picture-in-Picture Mode: Draggable, minimizable overlay that stays active while you play.

Highly Customizable:

Drag to move, Right Click to resize.

Adjustable opacity and color themes via a built-in picker.

Persistent Settings: Position, size, colors, and transparency are saved automatically.
![Color Picker](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9a812c6bfc7dc9baff586c1366f8b9846e4ee0b7_0.webp)

### 🎮 Controls (Default)
Home – Open/close browser

Middle-Click (Edit Mode) – Color/transparency picker

Left Mouse Button - Drags window

Right Mouse Button - Resizes window

### 🚀 Getting Started
Install MCEF, open the browser with Home, navigate normally, resize and position as needed, and minimize when not in use.

### 📋 Requirements
Minecraft 1.21.1–1.21.4 (NeoForge/Fabric)
MCEF 2.1.6+

### 🐛 Notes
Requires proper MCEF setup.
Some sites may not function due to Chromium restrictions.
Performance depends on website complexity.

📜 License
MIT
Developed by Mr_Zylr using MCEF
